close_reason: Correctness over performance. The NVM metadata round-trip validates the cache against concurrent mutation from other wolfHSM clients. Removing it would introduce silent stale-data bugs.
description: In secretx-wolfhsm/src/lib.rs, find_cached_or_scan issues an nvm_metadata round-trip to the wolfHSM server on every call, even when the cached NVM ID is still valid (lines 372-383). The comment says this is needed because 'another client may have deleted and recreated the object under a different NVM ID', but for the common single-client case this doubles the round-trip count per get()/refresh() call. The cache provides no latency benefit in steady state. Options: (1) trust the cached ID without revalidation and only re-scan on error (lazily), which is the standard cache pattern; (2) document explicitly that the cache only helps avoid the O(n) full NVM scan on first access, and rename accordingly. The current approach contradicts the intent described in the struct doc ('Avoids O(n) scan per call').
title: wolfhsm: cache validation round-trip on every get()
